input.headline: The World in Pictures Get The AP’s most compelling photographs sent directly to your inbox.

raw.headline: The World in Pictures Get The AP’s most compelling photographs sent directly to your inbox.

winner: I signed up for *The World in Pictures* because I wanted my inbox to feel like a postcard from every global disaster—you know, just for the aesthetic.

candidates.text: Why did the AP’s ‘World in Pictures’ photographer break up with his camera? It couldn’t *frame* their relationship properly!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'frame' are clever and specific to photography, with a surprise twist that fits the headline well."}

candidates.text: I tried subscribing to "The World in Pictures," but all I got was a box of blank canvases and a note saying, "You see what you want to.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on subscription expectations, concise and specific with a clever play on 'see what you want'"}

candidates.text: I clicked "World in Pictures" for a travel blog inspiration session and got images of war zones. I canceled my vacation savings plan and started investing in bulletproof vests.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with specific, relatable scenario and clever wordplay on 'vacation savings plan'"}

candidates.text: Why did the photographer break up with his camera? Because it kept sending him unflattering selfies without permission—just like *The World in Pictures*!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "Puns on 'The World in Pictures' with a relatable scenario; concise and specific, though the surprise is moderate."}

candidates.text: I signed up for *The World in Pictures* because I wanted my inbox to feel like a postcard from every global disaster—you know, just for the aesthetic.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific with a clever twist on the headline's promise."}
